initial_program:
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid dimensions
% ------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Each output cell receives exactly one colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Border colours (must be respected by constraints)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% top row  colour 2
:- output(R,C,Color), row(R), col(C), R = 0, Color != 2.
% bottom row  colour 8
:- output(R,C,Color), row(R), col(C), R = 11, Color != 8.
% left column  colour 8
:- output(R,C,Color), row(R), col(C), col(C), C = 0, Color != 8.
% right column  colour 2
:- output(R,C,Color), row(R), col(C), col(C), C = 11, Color != 2.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Central region (rows 3..8 and cols 3..8) must be 3
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% The four constraints below are applied to *every* cell.
% They forbid every colour except 3 for cells that satisfy the
% geometric condition R >= 3, R <= 8, C >= 3, C <= 8.
:- output(R,C,Color), row(R), col(C), R >= 3, R <= 8, C >= 3, C <= 8, Color != 0.
:- output(R,C,Color), row(R), col(C), R >= 3, R <= 8, C >= 3, C <= 8, Color != 2.
:- output(R,C,Color), row(R), col(C), R >= 3, R <= 8, C >= 3, C <= 8, Color != 8.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. All other cells (outside the central region and not on a border)
%    must be 0.  For those cells we forbid 2, 3 and 8.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- output(R,C,Color), row(R), col(C),
    R != 0, R != 11, C != 0, C != 11,
    (R >= 3, R <= 8, C >= 3, C <= 8),   % cells *outside* the central square
    Color != 2.
:- output(R,C,Color), row(R), col(C),
    R != 0, R != 11, C != 0, C != 11,
    (R >= 3, R <= 8, C >= 3, C <= 8),   % cells outside the central square but inside the
    (R != 3, R != 4, R != 5, R != 6, R != 7, R != 7, R != 8, C != 3, C != 4, C != 4,
     C != 5, C != 5, C != 6, C != 6, C != 7, C != 7, C != 8, C != 8), % the four corners of the
    Color != 3.                              % central square  they are not allowed to be 3

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Reveal only the output grid
% ------------------------------------------------------------
#show output/3.
